predictions.speaker: Mary Lou Kultgen
predictions.prediction: He's not going to make it. He's going to die before the end of this term.
predictions.context: Mary Lou is discussing Joe Biden running for re-election in 2024. She is expressing fear about what would happen if he died in office and Kamala Harris became president.
predictions.timeframe: before the end of his current term (by January 2025)

predictions.speaker: Bob Kultgen
predictions.prediction: This is not going to go away. Gun control is not going to make it go away. It's going to get worse.
predictions.context: Bob is arguing against gun control measures in response to a discussion about mass shootings in America. He believes the violence will increase regardless of policy.

predictions.speaker: Bob Kultgen
predictions.prediction: 2023 there will be more people murdered in Washington by guns than there were in 2022.
predictions.context: Bob is making a specific bet in response to Chad mentioning that Washington state recently banned certain types of guns. He claims the ban will lead to more gun murders.

predictions.speaker: Chad Kultgen
predictions.prediction: We're about to move into a phase of media generation really within the next six months. You're going to start to see a lot of insane shit.
predictions.context: Chad is introducing a topic about AI-generated media and deepfakes, predicting a rapid increase in their use and prevalence, especially in political campaigns.

predictions.speaker: Haley Popp
predictions.prediction: Within a year or two years, I don't think anybody will be able to determine [if AI-generated media is real or fake].
predictions.context: Haley is responding to Chad's question about whether people will be able to discern AI deepfakes from reality. She believes the technology will soon become indistinguishable.

predictions.speaker: Chad Kultgen
predictions.prediction: We're about to cross that threshold within a year. This is all happening now.
predictions.context: Chad is continuing the discussion on AI-generated media, asserting that society will soon reach a point where we cannot believe anything we see or hear due to perfect fakes.

predictions.speaker: Bob Kultgen
predictions.prediction: Fox is going to go broke.
predictions.context: Bob is discussing Tucker Carlson's departure from Fox News. He believes the network will fail financially as a result.

predictions.speaker: Bob Kultgen
predictions.prediction: He'll start his own [media platform]. And be more popular. He'll make more money on his own than he ever will working for a network.
predictions.context: Bob is predicting Tucker Carlson's career path after being fired from Fox News. He believes Carlson will start his own platform and be more successful.

predictions.speaker: Bob Kultgen
predictions.prediction: They're going to come up with a week deal and it's going to be nationwide.
predictions.context: Bob is arguing about abortion restrictions. He predicts that a specific nationwide gestational limit (in weeks) for abortion will be established.

predictions.speaker: Bob Kultgen
predictions.prediction: So far it has been [a losing battle], it won't be this time around.
predictions.context: Bob is responding to Chad's question about whether abortion is a losing battle for Republicans. He concedes it has been but predicts it will not be in the upcoming political cycle.
